journal_entry.description: Meal expense for employee working meeting paid by corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: The meal was for a working meeting among employees, which qualifies as a Meals and Entertainment expense under IFRS. In Canada, meals for business purposes are deductible expenses (though only 50% is deductible for tax purposes, the full amount is recorded as an expense with the limitation applied at tax filing). The corporate credit card creates a liability (Credit Card Payable) that will be settled later. No HST/GST is recoverable on meals and entertainment expenses in most cases as they are subject to the 50% limitation rule, but the HST paid should still be recorded as HST Receivable as it was paid. Assuming a typical tax rate, meals would include embedded tax.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

output.reason: The meal for a working meeting among employees is a business expense (meals & entertainment expense). This represents an expense increase, which is debited. The credit card payment method doesn't affect the debit classification - the debit is to the expense account.

output.description: Meal expense for employee working meeting paid by corporate credit card
output.rationale: The meal was for a working meeting among employees, which qualifies as a Meals and Entertainment expense under IFRS. In Canada, meals for business purposes are deductible expenses (though only 50% is deductible for tax purposes, the full amount is recorded as an expense with the limitation applied at tax filing). No HST/GST is recoverable on meals and entertainment expenses in most cases as they are subject to the 50% limitation rule, but the HST paid should still be recorded as HST Receivable as it was paid. Assuming a typical tax rate, meals would include embedded tax.

output.reason: Entry balances ($125 debit = $125 credit). Meals and Entertainment Expense is the correct account for employee working meeting meals. Credit Card Payable correctly records the liability. Amount matches transaction. The entry treats the $125 as inclusive of any embedded tax, which is a reasonable interpretation when tax breakdown is not specified. While the rationale mentions HST considerations, the practical recording of the gross amount to expense is acceptable for meals where tax may be embedded in the stated price.
